section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3
Commencing any Phase 8 activity before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional

check_reasoning.13.1: No references to 'Annex [0-9]', 'Grant Agreement Annex', 'AGA Template', or 'Model Grant Agreement template' found as structural authorities in the artifact. CC-10 correctly references 'Tier 2A application form af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf' as the structural authority for section ordering and completeness.
check_reasoning.13.2: The assembled draft is a structural assembly artifact that does not directly assert call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts. It references section-level artifacts that handle their own call traceability. No direct call requirement claims are made without Tier 2B traceability in this artifact.
check_reasoning.13.3: Project facts referenced in consistency checks (8 partners, 8 objectives OBJ-1 through OBJ-8) are traced to Tier 3 via traceability_footer.primary_sources which includes 'docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json' and 'docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json'.
check_reasoning.13.4: This is a Phase 8 artifact (schema_id: 'orch.tier5.part_b_assembled_draft.v1'). Budget gate assessment file at docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json is outside the declared input boundary for this skill invocation and cannot be directly verified. However, the artifact itself contains no budget-dependent content: no '€[0-9]+', '[0-9]+ EUR', or '[0-9]+ person-months' patterns found. CC-08 explicitly confirms 'no specific budget figures or person-month allocations appear in any section; Implementation references budget gate validation but no numerical values'. No budget-dependent claims are present in the artifact content.
check_reasoning.13.5: Not directly checkable from artifact content alone. This check is enforced by the decision-log-update skill at the agent execution level, not by inspecting deliverable artifacts.
check_reasoning.13.6: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. The assembled draft does not redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic.
check_reasoning.13.7: No evidence of phase reordering. The artifact references valid upstream inputs: Tier 3 project instantiation data and Tier 2A application form. Section artifacts referenced are all Phase 8 outputs. No circular or out-of-sequence phase references detected.
check_reasoning.13.8: Applicable as a Tier 5 deliverable. CC-12 reports validation statuses: Excellence=inferred, Impact=inferred, Implementation=confirmed. No section has overall_status='unresolved'. CC-11 confirms traceability footer consistency: no section combines no_unsupported_claims_declaration=true with overall_status=unresolved. Two sections carry 'inferred' status with specific inferred claims identified (Excellence CLM-06/CLM-07, Impact strategic-autonomy claim), which is a valid §12.2 status, not an unresolved gap.
check_reasoning.13.9: The artifact does not make direct regulatory claims about Horizon Europe participation conditions, grant agreement terms, or financial regulation requirements. CC-10 references 'Tier 2A application form af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf', which is a proper Tier 2A source reference, not generic programme knowledge.
check_reasoning.13.10: Applicable as a Tier 5 artifact. traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is true. primary_sources references Tier 5 section artifacts and Tier 3 project instantiation data. derivation_note explains the assembly relationship: 'Assembled draft inherits section-level proposal claim traceability from referenced section artifacts; this artifact adds only section index and cross-section consistency findings.' No claims without source references detected.
check_reasoning.13.11: Not checkable from a phase output or deliverable artifact. The artifact does not contain evidence of Tier 1 or Tier 2 source modification.
check_reasoning.13.12: Not directly checkable from an artifact. The artifact does not contain any statement treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional.
